channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: 7 Britons among 13 killed in Spain wildfire; grieving family says 'heartbroken'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UK proposes overnight social media curfew for 16, 17-year-olds
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: New England had almost completely lost its forests by the 1800s after centuries of logging. Then nature slowly reclaimed the land in one of history's greatest forest recoveries, covering about 60% of the region once again
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Congo's Ebola outbreak tops 2,000 cases, kills 754 in fastest-growing surge
item.description: In the Democratic Republic of Congo, the Ebola situation has worsened, now exceeding two thousand confirmed infections and recording 754 lives lost. This outbreak has become the most rapid on record, overwhelming health authorities. Reports of suspected cases in additional provinces suggest wider transmission, though new treatment trials targeting the Bundibugyo strain provide potential avenues for relief amid severe challenges in containment.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 7 pink lakes in Australia and the science behind their unusual colours
item.description: Australia features numerous pink lakes scattered across diverse landscapes. These lakes display shifting colours influenced by weather and water conditions. Western Australia's Hutt Lagoon and pink and rainbow lakes are notable examples. South Australia's Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre and Lake MacDonnell also showcase vibrant hues. Victoria's Murray-Sunset National Park adds to the country's collection of these natural wonders.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 6 most snake-filled water bodies in South Carolina: From the Savannah River to the ACE Basin
item.description: South Carolina's abundant waters attract many snake species, both harmless and dangerous. The Savannah River and Great Pee Dee River are home to several notable snake populations. Congaree River and Lake Murray host a variety of snakes, including venomous ones. Lake Hartwell and the ACE Basin also feature numerous water-dwelling snake species. These warm environments and plentiful prey make South Carolina ideal for snakes.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Forest City Fights Pollution:China is building a city where 40,000 trees will grow across buildings
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Andy Burnham condemns alleged right-wing terror plot at Islamic gathering, says 'hatred against Muslims has no place in UK'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Swiss cows wear giant bells for a simple reason rooted in mountain life
item.description: In the picturesque Alps, cowbells play a crucial role for farmers overseeing their herds. These melodic bells make it easier to track cattle across expansive mountain landscapes, especially amid fluctuating weather conditions. Local traditions like Alpabzug honor this custom, showcasing beautifully adorned bells as part of cattle returning home. Interestingly, studies suggest cows become accustomed to these sounds, influencing their behavior.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: How tiny pollen grains helped solve a brutal murder: The fascinating science behind nature's invisible crime scene evidence
item.description: Tiny pollen grains offer vital clues for investigators solving complex criminal cases. Forensic palynologists analyze these microscopic particles to link suspects to crime scenes. A notable murder case in New Zealand was solved using unusual mutated pollen evidence. Pollen's resistance to decomposition makes it a persistent and valuable forensic tool. This silent evidence helps identify and locate regions, aiding in criminal investigations.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: In Tokyo's hidden 1,100-year-old shrine, thousands of desperate fans are praying for impossible concert tickets
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Indian seafarer missing after attack off Oman coast confirmed dead
Florida treasure hunters searching a 400-year-old shipwreck discovered a rare 22.5-pound silver bar worth up to $100,000
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Stoic philosopher Epictetus: "Any person capable of angering you becomes your master; he can anger you only when…" - why losing control of your emotions can hand power to someone else
item.description: Stoic philosopher Epictetus taught that anger is a choice, not an automatic reaction. He believed individuals control their responses to external provocations. This ancient wisdom remains relevant in today's digital age. Modern life offers many opportunities for emotional entanglement with others. Recognizing this power dynamic helps maintain personal control over one's day.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists fitted a European honey buzzard with a GPS tracker. The bird flew for 42 days, covering more than 10,000 km from South Africa to Finland and helping researchers uncover the secrets of one of nature's greatest migrations
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Nepal court jails ex-deputy PM, home minister in fake refugee scam; 14 others convicted
item.description: Two former Nepal ministers received prison sentences for their involvement in a fake refugee document scam. Top Bahadur Rayamajhi was sentenced to four years, and Bal Krishna Khand received two years. Fourteen others were also convicted and sentenced for their roles in the alleged fraud. The investigation began in 2023 after authorities uncovered claims of Nepali citizens posing as refugees.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Middle East war: Trump weighs 'devastating strikes'; Iran targets US bases in Middle East - key points
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard has asserted that the Strait of Hormuz will stay closed until the United States changes its course of action. This comes on the heels of the US conducting new strikes near the Strait and reinstating a naval blockade. Iran has also announced attacks on US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain, exacerbating regional tensions after a recently collapsed truce.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Princess Diana: "I don't want expensive gifts; I don't want to be bought. I have everything I want. I just want someone to…" - why emotional security can matter more than wealth or luxury
item.description: Princess Diana emphasized that emotional security surpasses material possessions. She desired dependable presence and a feeling of safety from loved ones. This need for reassurance begins in childhood and continues throughout life. Genuine presence offers more to relationships than grand, extravagant gestures. Her words highlight the enduring human need for emotional connection and support.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: FBI adds Indian-origin man to most wanted list over alleged organised crime links
item.description: The FBI has added Nitish Kaushal to its most wanted list for his alleged role in organized crime. Kaushal is linked to the Jaggu Bhagwanpuria group involved in various illegal activities. This action follows charges against Lawrence Bishnoi and Goldy Brar for a Canadian assassination. Federal agents recently conducted large raids targeting the Lawrence Bishnoi network in the United States.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: US seismologist has been detained in China for nearly 2 years without trial: Family
item.description: A Chinese-born American seismologist faces spying charges in China. Youlin Chen has been detained for nearly two years, according to his wife. US officials have designated his detention as wrongful and a top priority. China denies any wrongful detention and states its judicial organs follow law. The case adds tension to the ongoing relationship between the two nuclear-armed nations.
